subject: Add support for mvn repos and proxy as parameters
commit_message:
Add support for mvn repos and proxy as parameters

Issue-ID: POLICY-2346
Signed-off-by: Bruno Sakoto <bruno.sakoto@bell.ca>
Change-Id: I8b2d7385cc752eb57ebd2b4e9786d71a46cb4733

entries.message:

Patch Set 2:

Hi Bruno, I ran some tests with your patch, not sure if does the right thing.   Note that in my original set up without your changes tests work behind a proxy, they take some time though as it is hitting timeouts.

With your patch, works the same but it is faster, I think still does not through proxy as I see connection errors:

[WARNING] Could not transfer metadata org.onap.oparent:version:3.0.0-SNAPSHOT/maven-metadata.xml from/to onap-snapshots (https://nexus.onap.org/content/repositories/snapshots/): Connection refused (Connection refused)
[WARNING] Could not transfer metadata org.onap.oparent:dependencies:3.0.0-SNAPSHOT/maven-metadata.xml from/to onap-snapshots (https://nexus.onap.org/content/repositories/snapshots/): Connection refused (Connection refused)

I looked at what your effective settings would look like with this:

 2001  export MAVEN_RELEASES_REPO_URL=https://nexus.onap.org/content/repositories/releases/
 2002  export MAVEN_PROXY_PROTOCOL=http
 2003  export MAVEN_PROXY_HOST=one.proxy.att.com
 2004  export MAVEN_PROXY_PORT=8080
 2005  export MAVEN_PROXY_NON_PROXY_HOSTS="localhost,127.0.*"
 2016  mvn help:effective-settings -s ./settings.xml

Here is the effective settings:

<settings xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/SETTINGS/1.1.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/SETTINGS/1.1.0 http://maven.apache.org/xsd/settings-1.1.0.xsd">
  <localRepository>/home/jhh/.m2/repository</localRepository>
  <proxies>
    <proxy>
      <port>0</port>
      <host>one.proxy.att.com</host>
      <nonProxyHosts>localhost,127.0.*</nonProxyHosts>
      <id>proxy-id</id>
    </proxy>
  </proxies>
  <profiles>
    <profile>
      <repositories>
        <repository>
          <releases />
          <snapshots>
            <enabled>false</enabled>
          </snapshots>
          <id>onap-releases</id>
          <name>onap-releases</name>
          <url>https://nexus.onap.org/content/repositories/releases/</url>
        </repository>
      </repositories>
      <id>onap-releases</id>
    </profile>
    <profile>
      <repositories>
        <repository>
          <releases>
            <enabled>false</enabled>
          </releases>
          <snapshots>
            <updatePolicy>always</updatePolicy>
          </snapshots>
          <id>onap-snapshots</id>
          <name>onap-snapshots</name>
          <url>https://nexus.onap.org/content/repositories/snapshots/</url>
        </repository>
      </repositories>
      <id>onap-snapshots</id>
    </profile>
  </profiles>
  <activeProfiles>
    <activeProfile>onap-releases</activeProfile>
    <activeProfile>onap-snapshots</activeProfile>
  </activeProfiles>
  <pluginGroups>
    <pluginGroup>org.apache.maven.plugins</pluginGroup>
    <pluginGroup>org.codehaus.mojo</pluginGroup>
  </pluginGroups>
</settings>

Note the <port> being set to 0.   I think that's the reason the connection closes right away and moves further but in reality it does not go through the proxy, that's my impression.

I also found this open ticket that seems quite related:

https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/MNG-6401

Patch Set 2:

More experiments that you may want to do, this works for me pretty quick (but not sure if related to have .m2 prepopulated already).   Worth give if a try.

Add to your settings.xml:   <offline>true</offline>

In your junit do:  
System.setProperty("kie.maven.offline.force", "true");

Give it a try with an empty .m2 cache to verify ..

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Being worked out with Bruno, not mergeable just yet.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Changing status to WIP to be back on this change when MNG-6401 will be fixed 

https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/MNG-6401
